metadata.title: Biostatistics Flow Debug Double-Check — Full Implementation (v3)
metadata.priority: P0 — validates complete biostatistics pipeline before production run
metadata.version-note: v3 corrects the factorial arithmetic to match the synthesis: - Full factorial: 4x3x2x3x2x5 = 720 conditions (NOT 24) - 24 is ONLY the training layer (Layer A). Post-training (Layer B: x3x2) and ensemble (Layer C: x5) multiply it. - Debug factorial: 4x3x2x2x2x4 = 384 conditions x 1 fold - ONLY Layer A runs on GPU (~$65). Layers B+C on local CPU (free). - Factorial YAML is COMPOSABLE via Hydra config groups. - ANOVA is LAYERED: core 3-way -> extended 5-way -> ensemble analysis separately. - Biostatistics auto-derives ALL factors from whichever factorial YAML was used (zero hardcoded factor names).

context.summary:
Verify and complete the biostatistics pipeline for the FULL 6-factor factorial experiment across 4 compute layers. The synthesis document (intermedia-plan-synthesis-pre-debug-run.md) defines the COMPLETE factorial as:
4 models x 3 losses x 2 aux_calib x 3 post-training x 2 recalibration x 5 ensemble = 720 CONDITIONS (production) or 384 conditions (debug)
Source of truth: docs/planning/pre-gcp-master-plan.xml line 16.
The 24-cell number that appears throughout earlier documents is ONLY Layer A (training). The full factorial multiplies Layer A x Layer B x Layer C: Layer A (Training, GPU): 4 x 3 x 2 = 24 cells [x fold_id] ~$65 cloud Layer B (Post-Training, CPU): x 3 post-training x 2 recalibration FREE (local) Layer C (Analysis, CPU): x 5 ensemble strategies FREE (local) Layer D (Biostatistics): analytical choices (spec curve) FREE (local)
COST MODEL: Only Layer A costs money on GPU (~$65 for 72 production runs on GCP L4 spot). Layers B and C run on the researcher's local CPU for free. This is a critical architectural advantage — the factorial explodes in conditions (720) but NOT in cloud cost.
The factorial YAML is COMPOSABLE via Hydra config groups. paper_full.yaml and debug.yaml are just TWO EXAMPLE named configs in configs/factorial/. A different lab could create my_lab.yaml with 2 models x 1 loss x 1 calib x 1 post-training x 1 recalib x 2 ensemble. The biostatistics flow must auto-derive ALL factors from whichever factorial YAML was used — zero hardcoded factor names.
The YAML structure is sectioned by layer: factors.training: model_family, loss_name, aux_calibration factors.post_training: method, recalibration factors.analysis: ensemble_strategy fixed: max_epochs, num_folds
Pipeline topology: Training (Flow 2) --> Post-Training (Flow 2.5) --> Analysis (Flow 3) --> Biostatistics (Flow 5b)
Key constraints: 1. Auto-derive ALL factors from factorial YAML (not hardcoded — zero factor names in code) 2. Query MLflow for all runs across training + post-training + evaluation experiments 3. Extract PER-VOLUME metrics (N=23 per fold = unit of analysis), not fold aggregates 4. ANOVA is LAYERED: core 3-way (model x loss x calib) -> extended 5-way (+post_training x recalib) -> ensemble analysis separately 5. K=1 debug uses per-volume replication; K=3 production uses fold as random effect 6. All statistical params from BiostatisticsConfig (CLAUDE.md Rule #29) 7. Specification curve iterates ALL Layer D analytical choices 8. Ensemble strategies ARE factorial factors for biostatistics (RESOLVED, not a question) 9. Platform paper (Nature Protocols) — demonstrate platform capability, not SOTA

composable-yaml.description:
The factorial design is COMPOSABLE via Hydra config groups. Users define their own factorial YAML in configs/factorial/. The platform provides paper_full.yaml and debug.yaml as examples. A different lab can create my_lab.yaml with any subset of factor levels.
The YAML structure is sectioned by layer: factors: training: # Layer A (GPU, cloud) model_family: [dynunet, mambavesselnet, sam3_topolora, sam3_hybrid] loss_name: [cbdice_cldice, dice_ce, dice_ce_cldice] aux_calibration: [true, false] post_training: # Layer B (CPU, local) method: [none, swa, multi_swa] recalibration: [none, temperature_scaling] analysis: # Layer C (CPU/GPU) ensemble_strategy: [none, per_loss_single_best, all_loss_single_best, per_loss_all_best, all_loss_all_best] fixed: max_epochs: 50 num_folds: 3
The biostatistics flow reads whichever factorial YAML was used and auto-derives ALL factor names and levels. Zero hardcoded factor names.

zero-shot-baselines.note: Evaluated but NOT trained in factorial grid

layer.name: Post-Training Factors
layer.description:
Applied to each trained checkpoint AFTER training. These are model-agnostic weight-space operations. No gradients flow. Each training run can produce MULTIPLE post-training variants.
Production: {none, swa, multi_swa} x {none, temperature_scaling} = 6 variants per training cell Debug: {none, swa} x {none, temperature_scaling} = 4 variants per training cell
This is WHERE the factorial explodes beyond 24 — but at ZERO additional cloud cost.

factors.factor: Full: none, swa, multi_swa (3 levels) Debug: none, swa (2 levels — multi_swa dropped for debug)
Full: none, temperature_scaling (2 levels) Debug: none, temperature_scaling (2 levels — same as full)

layer.expansion-rule: Each Layer A training run produces (post_training_method levels) x (recalibration levels) post-training variants: - Production: 72 training runs x 6 = 432 post-training variants - Debug: 24 training runs x 4 = 96 post-training variants These are NEW MLflow runs tagged with the method, inheriting all upstream Layer A tags.

mlflow-tags.tag: MLflow run_id of parent training run

layer.name: Analysis Factors
layer.description:
Ensemble predictions built from all training + post-training runs. Each ensemble strategy is a NEW factorial condition for Biostatistics. Ensemble averaging happens at the LOGIT level (average probability maps, then threshold, then compute metrics). NOT: compute per-member metrics, then average.
Production: 5 ensemble strategies (none + 4) Debug: 4 ensemble strategies (drop 'none' for debug)

factors.factor: Full: none, per_loss_single_best, all_loss_single_best, per_loss_all_best, all_loss_all_best (5 levels) Debug: per_loss_single_best, all_loss_single_best, per_loss_all_best, all_loss_all_best (4 levels — 'none' dropped)

layer.name: Biostatistics Analytical Choices
layer.description: These are NOT experimental factors — they are analytical choices (researcher degrees of freedom). The specification curve analysis systematically varies them to demonstrate how conclusions change (Simonsohn et al., 2020).

analytical-choices.choice: clDice, MASD, DSC, HD95, ASSD, NSD, BE_0, BE_1

production.formula: 4 x 3 x 2 x 3 x 2 x 5 = 720 conditions x 3 folds = 2160 evaluations
production.layer-a: 4 x 3 x 2 = 24 training cells x 3 folds = 72 GPU runs (~$65)
production.layer-b: 72 x 3 x 2 = 432 post-training variants (FREE, local CPU)
production.layer-c: 432 x 5 = 2160 evaluation runs (FREE, local CPU for DynUNet)

debug.formula: 4 x 3 x 2 x 2 x 2 x 4 = 384 conditions x 1 fold = 384 evaluations
debug.layer-a: 4 x 3 x 2 = 24 training cells x 1 fold = 24 GPU runs (~$5-10)
debug.layer-b: 24 x 2 x 2 = 96 post-training variants (FREE, local CPU)
debug.layer-c: 96 x 4 = 384 evaluation runs (FREE, local CPU for DynUNet)

full-factorial-totals.cost-model: ONLY Layer A runs on GPU (cloud). Layers B + C run on local CPU (FREE). The factorial explodes in conditions (720 production / 384 debug) but NOT in cloud cost (~$65 production / ~$5-10 debug).

unit-of-analysis.description: Per-volume metrics are the unit of analysis (N=23 per fold for MiniVess val). This enables paired statistical tests (Wilcoxon signed-rank), per-volume specification curve analysis, and bootstrap confidence intervals.
unit-of-analysis.debug-mode: K=1 fold: per-volume replication (N=23 volumes) provides the replication units for ANOVA and pairwise tests. No fold random effect needed.
unit-of-analysis.production-mode: K=3 folds: fold as random effect in mixed model. Per-volume scores nested within folds. N = 23 volumes x 3 folds = 69 observations per condition.

metric.name: clDice
metric.rationale: Centerline topology preservation for tubular structures

metric.name: MASD
metric.rationale: Mean Average Surface Distance — boundary accuracy

foil.rationale: Included to demonstrate misleading ranking for tubular structures — rank inversion IS a finding

mvrd-scope.note: Minimum Viable Research Design — debug pipeline validation
mvrd-scope.full-factorial: 720 conditions x 3 folds = 2160 evaluations (production) 384 conditions x 1 fold = 384 evaluations (debug)
mvrd-scope.training: 4 models x 3 losses x 2 calibrations x 1 fold x 2 epochs = 24 GPU runs
mvrd-scope.post-training: 24 runs x {none, swa} x {none, temperature_scaling} = 96 variants (local CPU, FREE)
mvrd-scope.analysis: 96 variants x 4 ensemble strategies = 384 evaluations + 2 zero-shot baselines (local CPU, FREE)
mvrd-scope.biostatistics: Layered ANOVA on ALL 6 factors from Layers A+B+C + spec curve + rank stability + calibration metrics

scope-reductions.note: ONLY these reductions allowed (Rule #27) — ALL 6 factorial dimensions present, fewer levels
scope-reductions.reduction: Post-training levels: 2 (not 3) — {none, swa} instead of {none, swa, multi_swa}

mvrd-scope.everything-else-identical: All 4 models, all 3 losses, both calibration levels, recalibration (2 levels), all remaining ensemble strategies (4), all zero-shot baselines, all statistical tests, all analytical choices. Debug = full production with less training and fewer factor levels, NOT less testing.

phase.name: Data Availability Verification (All 4 Layers, All 6 Factors)
phase.description:
Verify that the biostatistics flow can access ALL required data from MLflow across ALL four layers and ALL 6 experimental factors. Test both Cloud Run API path and local mlruns path. Write tests FIRST (TDD RED), then fix any gaps.
Key point: the biostatistics flow must auto-derive factor names from the factorial YAML (configs/factorial/*.yaml), NOT from hardcoded lists. The factor structure is: factors.training: model_family, loss_name, aux_calibration factors.post_training: method, recalibration factors.analysis: ensemble_strategy

tasks.task:
Write test: MLflow run discovery finds all Layer A factorial conditions. File: tests/v2/unit/biostatistics/test_data_availability.py
Test that discover_source_runs() can find N runs (where N matches the factorial design: model_family x loss_name x aux_calibration x folds).
Must verify: - Each run has model_family tag - Each run has with_aux_calib tag - Each run has fold_id - Each run has loss_function tag (note: tag mismatch B3 — training logs loss_name but ensemble builder reads loss_function) - Completeness: all 24 cells x K folds present

Write test: MLflow run discovery finds all Layer B post-training variants.
Test that discover_source_runs() also finds post-training runs tagged with: - post_training_method (none, swa, multi_swa) - recalibration (none, temperature_scaling) - upstream_training_run_id (links back to parent Layer A run) - ALL inherited Layer A tags (model_family, loss_name, fold_id, with_aux_calib)
For debug: 24 training x 4 variants = 96 post-training runs expected. For production: 72 training x 6 variants = 432 post-training runs expected.

Write test: DuckDB schema has ALL 6 factorial columns (Layers A+B+C). Verify the runs table includes: - Layer A: model_family, loss_name, with_aux_calib, fold_id - Layer B: post_training_method, recalibration - Layer C: ensemble_strategy Verify eval_metrics has per-volume rows (not just aggregates). Verify test_metrics has DeepVess rows.

Write test: per-volume metrics are queryable from DuckDB. For each condition (across all 6 factors from all layers), can we get individual volume scores for clDice, MASD, DSC? These are needed for Wilcoxon and spec curve. Unit of analysis = per-volume (N=23 per fold), NOT per-fold aggregates.

Write test: zero-shot baselines (sam3_vanilla, vesselfm) are discoverable. These are NOT part of the training factorial grid but must be included in the biostatistics analysis. Verify they can be queried from MLflow and distinguished from trained models via tags.

Write test: all statistical parameters come from BiostatisticsConfig. Verify NO hardcoded alpha, seed, ROPE values, or metric names in biostatistics_statistics.py, biostatistics_specification_curve.py, or biostatistics_flow.py. All must read from config objects.

phase.gate: All data availability tests written. Identify which FAIL across all 4 layers and all 6 factors.

phase.name: Fix Data Pipeline Gaps (All 4 Layers, All 6 Factors)
phase.description: Fix all data gaps identified in Phase 0. Ensure all 6 factorial factor tags (from Layers A, B, and C) are discoverable and stored in DuckDB. Ensure per-volume metrics are the unit of analysis.

tasks.task:
Add ALL 6 factorial columns to SourceRun and DuckDB schema.
File: src/minivess/pipeline/biostatistics_types.py ADD to SourceRun: - model_family: str (Layer A, factor 1) - with_aux_calib: bool (Layer A, factor 3) - post_training_method: str | None (Layer B, factor 4) - recalibration: str | None (Layer B, factor 5) - ensemble_strategy: str | None (Layer C, factor 6)
File: src/minivess/pipeline/biostatistics_duckdb.py ADD to _DDL_RUNS: - model_family VARCHAR - with_aux_calib BOOLEAN - post_training_method VARCHAR DEFAULT 'none' - recalibration VARCHAR DEFAULT 'none' - ensemble_strategy VARCHAR DEFAULT NULL

Update discovery to read ALL 6 factor tags from MLflow.
File: src/minivess/pipeline/biostatistics_discovery.py In discover_source_runs(): - Extract model_family from run tags (Layer A, factor 1) - Extract loss_name from run tags (Layer A, factor 2) - Extract with_aux_calib from run tags (Layer A, factor 3) - Extract post_training_method from run tags (Layer B, factor 4, default "none") - Extract recalibration from run tags (Layer B, factor 5, default "none") - Extract ensemble_strategy from run tags (Layer C, factor 6, default None) - Handle tag mismatch B3: fallback chain for loss_name vs loss_function
Discovery must query MULTIPLE MLflow experiments: - minivess_training (Layer A runs) - minivess_post_training (Layer B runs) - minivess_evaluation (Layer C runs)

Add auto-parsing of factorial config from composable YAML.
File: src/minivess/config/biostatistics_config.py Add: parse_factorial_design(yaml_path) -> FactorialDesign dataclass with: - training_factors: dict (from factors.training section) - post_training_factors: dict (from factors.post_training section) - analysis_factors: dict (from factors.analysis section) - all_factor_names: list[str] (auto-derived from all 3 sections) - expected_conditions: int (product of ALL factor levels across all layers) - layer_a_conditions: int (product of training factors only — this is what costs GPU) - metric_hierarchy: MetricHierarchy (co-primary, foil, secondary)
This must work for ANY factorial YAML, not just paper_full or debug. A lab creating my_lab.yaml with 2x1x1x1x1x2 = 4 conditions must work.

Verify per-volume metrics are logged by analysis_flow. Check that eval_metrics DuckDB table contains individual volume scores (eval/{fold_id}/vol/{volume_id}/{metric}), not just fold-level aggregates. This is the unit of analysis.

Add zero-shot baseline handling to discovery. sam3_vanilla and vesselfm are evaluated but not trained in the factorial grid. They must be discoverable and tagged as is_zero_shot=true so biostatistics can: (a) include them in overall ranking analysis (b) exclude them from factorial ANOVA (they have no loss/calib/post-training factors) (c) include them in pairwise comparisons against trained models (d) include them in specification curve as additional conditions

Add external test set (DeepVess) discovery. Biostatistics must analyze BOTH trainval (MiniVess CV) and test (DeepVess) splits separately, as decided in QA round 4. The discovery must find test/deepvess/* metrics and store them in a separate DuckDB table or with a split column.

phase.gate: All Phase 0 tests GREEN across all 4 layers and all 6 factors.

phase.name: Implement Layered N-Way Factorial ANOVA
phase.description:
Implement the LAYERED ANOVA structure that matches the 4-layer factorial:
ANOVA Phase 1 — Core 3-way (Layer A factors only): model_family x loss_name x aux_calibration Available immediately after Training Flow completes. Answers: "Which model/loss/calibration combination is best?"
ANOVA Phase 2 — Extended 5-way (Layer A + B factors): model_family x loss_name x aux_calibration x post_training_method x recalibration Available after Post-Training Flow completes. Answers: "Does SWA/temperature scaling improve results? Interaction with model choice?"
ANOVA Phase 3 — Ensemble analysis (Layer C factor, analyzed separately): ensemble_strategy analyzed separately because ensemble conditions are NOT independent of training conditions (ensembles are COMPOSED FROM training runs). Answers: "Which ensemble strategy performs best? Does it interact with model family?"
The factor list is auto-derived from the factorial YAML — NOT hardcoded to 3 or 5 or 6. The layered structure matches the progressive availability of upstream flow results.
K=1 debug: per-volume replication (N=23). K=3 production: fold as random effect.

tasks.task:
Write test: Layered ANOVA produces correct results at each phase.
ANOVA Phase 1 — Core 3-way (Layer A: model x loss x calib): - 3 main effects: model_family, loss_name, aux_calibration - 3 two-way interactions: model*loss, model*calib, loss*calib - 1 three-way interaction: model*loss*calib - Partial eta-squared and omega-squared for each
ANOVA Phase 2 — Extended 5-way (Layer A+B: +post_training x recalib): - 5 main effects - 10 two-way interactions - 10 three-way interactions - All effect sizes computed
ANOVA Phase 3 — Ensemble analysis (Layer C): - ensemble_strategy main effect - ensemble_strategy x model_family interaction - Separate from Phases 1-2 because ensembles are composed from training runs
All parameters from BiostatisticsConfig (alpha, seed, etc.). Factor names auto-derived from factorial YAML, not hardcoded.

Verify compute_factorial_anova() handles the layered approach.
File: src/minivess/pipeline/biostatistics_statistics.py The implementation must accept factor_names: list[str] derived from config and build the formula dynamically. Verify it works for: - Phase 1: 3 factors (Layer A only) — core ANOVA, always available - Phase 2: 5 factors (Layers A + B) — extended after post-training - Phase 3: ensemble_strategy analyzed separately with selected covariates Ensure factor_names comes from parsed factorial YAML, never hardcoded.

For debug run (K=1 fold): verify per-volume replication works. When num_folds=1, per-volume scores (N=23) are the replication units. Verify the existing K=1 fallback in compute_factorial_anova() correctly uses standard ANOVA with per-volume data, not mixed model. Must work for all 3 ANOVA phases (3-way, 5-way, ensemble).

For production run (K=3 folds): verify fold as random effect. When num_folds=3, fold is a random effect (mixed model). Verify pingouin/statsmodels handles N-way with fold as random effect. Note: pingouin.mixed_anova only supports 1 between + 1 within, so for 3+ between factors, document the statistical approach taken. Must work for all 3 ANOVA phases.

phase.gate: Layered ANOVA returns correct structure for synthetic test data at all 3 phases (3-way, 5-way, ensemble).

phase.name: Implement Specification Curve Analysis (All Layer D Choices)
phase.description:
Full specification curve across ALL Layer D researcher degrees of freedom: metric choice, aggregation method, test_type, effect_size measure, MCC_method, fold_subset, ROPE width, alpha level.
The specification curve iterates ALL analytical choices from BiostatisticsConfig to demonstrate how conclusions change across researcher degrees of freedom (Simonsohn et al., 2020).

tasks.task: Write test: specification curve iterates ALL Layer D choices. For the debug factorial design (384 conditions), the specification space should include: - 8 metrics x 2 aggregation x 4 test_types x 3 effect_sizes = 192 base - With condition pairs: 192 x C(n_conditions, 2) total specifications All parameters from BiostatisticsConfig.

Verify specification curve engine handles expanded Layer D choices.
File: src/minivess/pipeline/biostatistics_specification_curve.py The current implementation iterates metric x aggregation x condition_pair. Extend to also iterate: - test_type: wilcoxon, friedman, bayesian_signed_rank, bootstrap - effect_size: cohens_d, cliffs_delta, vda - MCC_method: holm_bonferroni, bh_fdr - fold_subset: all, leave-one-out (production only, skip for K=1)
All choice levels read from BiostatisticsConfig, not hardcoded.
Wire expanded specification curve into biostatistics_flow.py. Verify it is wired as a Prefect task after pairwise comparisons. Verify Layer D choices are passed from BiostatisticsConfig.
Specification curve for external test set (DeepVess). Run a separate specification curve for test/deepvess metrics. This demonstrates the platform handles arbitrary test datasets.

phase.gate: Specification curve generates correct figure with synthetic data, iterating all Layer D choices.

phase.name: Implement Rank Stability + Calibration + Derived Metrics
phase.description: Compute rank stability index (does metric choice change model ranking?), calibration assessment, and other derived metrics. All must handle ALL 6 factorial factors from Layers A+B+C, not just Layer A alone.

tasks.task:
Implement rank stability analysis across all 720/384 conditions. For each pair of metrics (clDice vs DSC, MASD vs DSC, etc.): - Compute Kendall's tau between model rankings - Test significance of rank agreement - The clDice vs DSC rank inversion IS a paper finding
Rankings must be computed across ALL Layer A+B+C conditions (not just model_family alone). A post-training method that improves DSC ranking but not clDice ranking is a finding. An ensemble strategy that changes rank order is a finding.

Implement calibration metrics (Brier score, O/E ratio, IPA). Read from post-training flow calibration artifacts (Layer B). Compare calibrated vs uncalibrated variants (recalibration factor). Already partially implemented — verify and extend.

Implement Bayesian analysis upgrades. Use baycomp for Bayesian signed-rank with ROPE. ROPE values from BiostatisticsConfig.rope_values (not hardcoded). Already partially implemented — verify and extend.
Bootstrap ranking instability (Riley et al.) across all conditions. Verify compute_riley_instability() handles conditions from all 6 factors. Use n_bootstrap from BiostatisticsConfig.n_bootstrap.

phase.name: Layer B + C Factor Integration into Layered ANOVA
phase.description:
Ensure that post-training (Layer B) and ensemble (Layer C) factors are treated as first-class ANOVA factors in the layered structure. The synthesis explicitly states: "Ensemble strategies are factorial factors for biostatistics (NOT a question — resolved)"
The layered ANOVA from Phase 2 progressively includes these factors as upstream flows complete. This phase tests the integration end-to-end.

tasks.task: Write test: ANOVA Phase 2 includes Layer B factors. Create synthetic data with conditions spanning all 5 factors: e.g., dynunet__dice_ce__calibTrue__swa__temp_scaling Verify that compute_factorial_anova() with factor_names auto-derived from factors.training + factors.post_training sections produces: - 5 main effects - 10 two-way interactions - All effect sizes computed

Write test: ANOVA Phase 3 includes Layer C ensemble_strategy. Ensemble is analyzed separately because ensembles are composed from training runs (not independent). Verify that conditions like: dynunet__dice_ce__calibTrue__swa__none__per_loss_single_best produce valid separate ANOVA results with ensemble_strategy as a factor cross-tabulated with selected Layer A covariates.

Implement progressive factor inclusion (matches layered ANOVA structure). The biostatistics flow should support running ANOVA in stages: 1. Phase 1: Layer A only (3-way — always available after training) 2. Phase 2: Layer A + B (5-way — after post-training flow runs) 3. Phase 3: Layer C separately (ensemble analysis — after analysis flow)
The factor_names list is auto-derived from the factorial YAML's section structure (factors.training, factors.post_training, factors.analysis). Which phases run is determined by which upstream data is available.

Handle unbalanced designs gracefully. When Layer B or C factors create unbalanced cells (e.g., some models have SWA variants, others don't), the ANOVA must: - Use Type III sum of squares (already implemented via statsmodels) - Log a warning about unbalanced cells - Still produce valid results

phase.gate: Layered ANOVA produces correct results for Phase 1 (3-way), Phase 2 (5-way), and Phase 3 (ensemble).

phase.name: Zero-Shot Baseline Analysis
phase.description: Handle sam3_vanilla and vesselfm zero-shot baselines in the biostatistics pipeline. These models are NOT part of the 6-factor factorial grid but must be included in ranking and pairwise analysis.

tasks.task: Write test: zero-shot baselines are included in pairwise comparisons. sam3_vanilla vs each trained model on MiniVess metrics. vesselfm vs each trained model on DeepVess metrics ONLY.

Implement zero-shot baseline analysis. - Exclude zero-shot models from factorial ANOVA (they lack loss/calib/post-training factors) - Include in overall model ranking (rank stability, bootstrap) - Include in pairwise Wilcoxon comparisons vs trained models - Include in specification curve as additional conditions - Flag vesselfm as DeepVess-only (MiniVess evaluation = data leakage)
Add is_zero_shot tag handling in SourceRun and DuckDB schema. SourceRun gains: is_zero_shot: bool = False DuckDB runs table gains: is_zero_shot BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE

phase.name: TRIPOD+AI Preregistration Mapping
phase.description: Map each statistical test to TRIPOD+AI checklist items. Creates a de facto preregistration document.

tasks.task:
Create preregistration mapping document. File: docs/planning/preregistration-tripod-mapping.md
Map: - TRIPOD 3 (Study design) -> 6-factor layered factorial design YAML (720 conditions) - TRIPOD 6 (Sample size) -> 70 volumes, 3-fold, power analysis, N=23 per fold - TRIPOD 7 (Outcome) -> clDice + MASD (MetricsReloaded) as co-primary - TRIPOD 10 (Statistical methods) -> Layered ANOVA (3-way core -> 5-way extended -> ensemble), Wilcoxon, spec curve - TRIPOD 16 (Model performance) -> per-volume + per-fold + external test + ensemble - TRIPOD-AI items -> calibration (Layer B recalibration factor), uncertainty (Layer C ensembles), fairness
Must cover ALL 4 layers and ALL 6 experimental factors.
Wire TRIPOD mapping into biostatistics_flow output. The flow should produce a TRIPOD compliance checklist as a LaTeX table or structured artifact, not just a planning doc.

phase.name: External Test Set (DeepVess) Analysis
phase.description: Run biostatistics analysis on external test set separately. The synthesis specifies both trainval AND test splits (QA round 4). This demonstrates the PLATFORM'S ability to handle arbitrary test datasets.

tasks.task: Write test: biostatistics flow produces separate analysis for DeepVess. Verify that the flow runs layered ANOVA, pairwise, spec curve, and rank stability on test/deepvess/* metrics independently from trainval metrics.

Implement split-aware analysis. BiostatisticsConfig.splits = ["trainval", "test"] (already configured). For each split: - Discover runs with metrics for that split - Run full statistical analysis pipeline (all 3 ANOVA phases) - Generate separate figures and tables - Compare trainval vs test rankings (generalization analysis)

Train/test comparison analysis. Compare model rankings on trainval (MiniVess) vs test (DeepVess). Kendall's tau between trainval and test rankings indicates how well cross-validated performance predicts external test performance. This is a key platform capability demonstration.

phase.name: Integration Test with Synthetic Data (Full 6-Factor, 4-Layer)
phase.description:
Run the complete biostatistics pipeline with synthetic MLflow data that mimics the FULL 6-factor, 4-layer factorial design output. Verify end-to-end.
Synthetic data must include ALL layers: - Layer A: 24 training conditions x 1 fold - Layer B: 24 x {none, swa} x {none, temp_scaling} = 96 post-training variants - Layer C: 96 x 4 ensemble strategies = 384 evaluation conditions - Zero-shot: 2 baselines Total: 384 + 2 = 386 conditions with per-volume metrics

tasks.task:
Create synthetic MLflow fixture covering all 6 factors across 4 layers.
Layer A: 24 conditions x 1 fold with random per-volume metrics Layer B: 24 x {none, swa} x {none, temp_scaling} = 96 post-training variants Layer C: 96 x 4 ensemble strategies = 384 evaluation conditions Zero-shot: 2 baselines (sam3_vanilla on minivess, vesselfm on deepvess)
Write to a tmp mlruns directory. Per-volume metrics for clDice, MASD, DSC at minimum. Include test/deepvess/* metrics for external test analysis.
Factor columns in synthetic data must match the factorial YAML structure: factors.training keys + factors.post_training keys + factors.analysis keys.

Run biostatistics_flow() against full synthetic data. Verify: - DuckDB populated with ALL 6 factorial columns from Layers A+B+C - Layered ANOVA computed: * Phase 1: 3-way (model x loss x calib) * Phase 2: 5-way (+post_training x recalib) * Phase 3: ensemble analysis separately - Pairwise comparisons done (Wilcoxon + 3 effect sizes + 2-tier MCC) - Specification curve generated (ALL Layer D choices iterated) - Rank stability computed (Kendall's tau across metric pairs) - Calibration metrics computed (Brier, O/E, IPA) - Bayesian ROPE analysis completed - TRIPOD mapping produced - Figures created (ANOVA, spec curve, rank stability, calibration) - LaTeX tables exported - Zero-shot baselines analyzed separately - DeepVess test metrics analyzed separately - DuckDB queryable for all 384 + 2 conditions
Run make test-staging to verify no regressions.

Verify all statistical parameters come from BiostatisticsConfig. Grep for hardcoded alpha=0.05, seed=42, or metric names in: - biostatistics_statistics.py - biostatistics_specification_curve.py - biostatistics_calibration.py - biostatistics_flow.py All must read from config objects.

phase.gate: Full biostatistics pipeline produces correct output on 6-factor, 4-layer synthetic data (384 conditions).

report.relation: authoritative synthesis — this XML must be consistent with it

config.relation: Layer D choices + all statistical params

appendix.title: Factorial Arithmetic — Why 720, Not 24
appendix.description:
The number "24" has been repeatedly confused with the FULL factorial across 6+ sessions. This appendix makes the arithmetic unambiguous.
See: .claude/metalearning/2026-03-20-full-factorial-is-not-24-cells.md

breakdown.layer-a: 4 models x 3 losses x 2 aux_calib = 24 TRAINING cells x 3 folds (production) = 72 GPU runs x 1 fold (debug) = 24 GPU runs COST: ~$65 production, ~$5-10 debug
breakdown.layer-b: Production: 72 x {none, swa, multi_swa} x {none, temp_scaling} = 72 x 6 = 432 variants Debug: 24 x {none, swa} x {none, temp_scaling} = 24 x 4 = 96 variants COST: $0 (local CPU)
breakdown.layer-c: Production: 432 x {none, per_loss_single_best, all_loss_single_best, per_loss_all_best, all_loss_all_best} = 432 x 5 = 2160 evals Debug: 96 x {per_loss_single_best, all_loss_single_best, per_loss_all_best, all_loss_all_best} = 96 x 4 = 384 evals COST: $0 (local CPU for DynUNet; GPU for SAM3 inference but reuses existing weights)
breakdown.totals: FULL FACTORIAL (production): 4 x 3 x 2 x 3 x 2 x 5 = 720 conditions x 3 folds = 2160 evaluations DEBUG FACTORIAL: 4 x 3 x 2 x 2 x 2 x 4 = 384 conditions x 1 fold = 384 evaluations CLOUD COST: Only Layer A (72/24 GPU runs). Everything else is FREE on local CPU.

appendix.common-confusion: "24 conditions" = Layer A training cells ONLY. "720 conditions" = FULL factorial across all 6 experimental factors. "384 conditions" = DEBUG factorial (all 6 dimensions, fewer levels per dimension). The confusion arises because only Layer A costs GPU money. But for ANOVA and biostatistics, the full 720/384 conditions are what matter — every post-training variant and every ensemble strategy is a separate condition with its own per-volume metrics.

appendix.title: Layered ANOVA Structure
appendix.description: The ANOVA is NOT a single 6-way analysis. It is LAYERED to match the progressive availability of upstream flow results and the non-independence of ensemble conditions.

phase.name: Extended 5-way ANOVA (Layer A + B)
phase.question: Does SWA/temperature scaling improve results? Interaction with model choice?

phase.name: Ensemble analysis (Layer C, separate)
phase.factors: ensemble_strategy (analyzed separately, crossed with selected Layer A covariates)
phase.question: Which ensemble strategy performs best? Does it interact with model family?
phase.note: Analyzed SEPARATELY because ensemble conditions are NOT independent of training conditions — ensembles are COMPOSED FROM training runs. A full 6-way ANOVA would violate independence assumptions. Instead, ensemble strategies are analyzed with selected Layer A factors as covariates.

appendix.title: Cross-Reference Analysis: Synthesis vs XML Versions
appendix.description: Systematic comparison of intermedia-plan-synthesis-pre-debug-run.md against XML versions. v3 corrects the full factorial arithmetic and layered ANOVA.

finding.title: Full factorial is 720 conditions, NOT 24
finding.synthesis-ref: Part 1.3: "4 x 3 x 2 x 3 x 2 x 5 = 720 conditions"
finding.xml-v2-state: v2 had 4-layer structure but did not state the 720 total or 384 debug total clearly
finding.resolution: v3 adds full-factorial-totals section, factorial-arithmetic appendix, and corrects all references

finding.title: Composable Hydra factorial YAML not described
finding.synthesis-ref: Part 1.3: "The factorial design is COMPOSABLE via Hydra config groups"
finding.xml-v2-state: No mention of composable YAML or that paper_full/debug are just example named configs
finding.resolution: v3 adds composable-yaml section with YAML structure and named-configs

finding.title: ANOVA described as "N-way expandable" but not LAYERED
finding.synthesis-ref: Part 1.4: "ANOVA: Layered — core 3-way -> extended 5-way -> ensemble analysis"
finding.xml-v2-state: Phase 2 says "N-Way Expandable" but treats it as a single growing ANOVA, not 3 layered phases
finding.resolution: v3 restructures to 3 ANOVA phases matching progressive upstream availability + independence constraints

finding.title: Cost model not stated — only Layer A costs GPU money
finding.synthesis-ref: Part 1.3: "ONLY Layer A runs on GPU (72 cloud runs ~$65). Layers B+C run on local CPU (free)"
Part 7.1: "Cost: ONLY Layer A runs on GPU"
finding.resolution: v3 adds cost attributes to each layer and cost-model section in full-factorial-totals

finding.title: Auto-derive factor names from factorial YAML (zero hardcoded)
finding.synthesis-ref: Part 1.3: "Users define their own factorial YAML" + Part 5 Pattern 2
finding.xml-v2-state: Tasks mention "not hardcoded" but don't describe the YAML section structure
finding.resolution: v3 adds factors.training / factors.post_training / factors.analysis YAML structure throughout

finding.title: Debug scope reductions incomplete — missing level reductions
finding.xml-v2-state: scope-reductions only listed 3 items (epochs, data, folds) — missing level reductions
finding.resolution: v3 adds 2 more scope-reductions: post-training levels and ensemble levels

finding.title: Layer B factor naming: calibration_method vs recalibration
finding.xml-v2-state: Used "calibration_method" which conflicts with Layer A "aux_calibration"
finding.resolution: v3 uses "recalibration" consistently for Layer B, "aux_calibration" for Layer A

finding.title: Ensemble analyzed SEPARATELY due to non-independence
finding.synthesis-ref: Part 1.4: "ensemble analysis separately" (not part of 5-way ANOVA)
finding.xml-v2-state: Phase 5 suggested adding ensemble as a 6th ANOVA factor alongside Layers A+B
finding.resolution: v3 makes ensemble a separate ANOVA phase (Phase 3) because ensembles are composed from training runs

finding.title: Per-volume is unit of analysis (N=23), not fold aggregates
finding.synthesis-ref: Part 1.2 LAYER A: "3-fold CV seed=42, 47 train / 23 val per fold"
finding.xml-v1-state: Mentioned in tasks but not structurally enforced
finding.resolution: Added explicit unit-of-analysis section, reinforced in T0.5, T2.3

finding.title: Zero-shot baseline analysis missing from v1
External test set (DeepVess) analysis as separate split missing from v1
finding.resolution: Phase 8 (External Test Set Analysis) with T8.1-T8.3

finding.title: K=1 debug replication method confirmed
finding.resolution: unit-of-analysis section with debug-mode and production-mode subsections

finding.title: BiostatisticsConfig parameter compliance test
finding.synthesis-ref: Part 3.3: "All statistical parameters read from BiostatisticsConfig"
finding.resolution: T0.8 (config compliance test) and T9.4 (hardcoded parameter grep)

appendix.title: Decision-Making Record (verbatim Q&A, retained from v1+v2)
appendix.user-prompt: After the push, let's double-check that our Biostatistic Flow gets all the data that it needs to do all the tests needed for our factorial design experiment. We can this in broader sense as well as we have not preregistered this study at all, and explore more statistical tests than we so much implemented. Focus on testing the pipeline mechanics to make sure that the biostatistic flow gets the data that it needs to compute whatever needs. Test the availability of the data to implement new statistical test for all possible stats that you can think of to make sure that you would have the data from MLflow on GCP from Evals!

qa-round.question: ANOVA design: 3-way factorial or 2-way + separate AuxCalib?
qa-round.answer: We have a lot more factors in our design! You really need to read the sources better - the kg should tell you the factor design! Your options are so bad that I feel that you did not bother to read the sources at all?
qa-round.lesson: ALWAYS read the full KG line-by-line before asking questions about the experimental design. The factors are defined in configs/hpo/paper_factorial.yaml and the KG decisions, not in assumptions.

qa-round.question: DuckDB schema gaps: add model_family and with_aux_calib?
qa-round.answer: This factorial design should be automatic, and the factors should be configured on the experiment-specific YAML which is only place where this is defined which then defines the downstream artifact generation! We are developing this repo for a platform/MLOps manuscript instead of SOTA science.
qa-round.lesson: The biostatistics flow must be GENERIC and config-driven. Factors come from the experiment YAML, not hardcoded column names. The platform IS the contribution.

qa-round.question: Primary metrics: val/dice?
qa-round.answer: clDice and MASD are the ground truth metrics, both at equal weight. Where did you get Dice as primary metric? You need to read the whole knowledge-graph line-by-line!
qa-round.lesson: Primary metrics are clDice + MASD (co-primary, MetricsReloaded-driven). DSC is a FOIL metric. The rank inversion between DSC and clDice IS a finding.

qa-round.question: Separate analyses for trainval AND test splits?
Data source: Cloud Run API or local sync?
XML scope: verification only or full implementation?
Derived metrics scope?
qa-round.answer: All in biostats — rank stability, spec curve p-values, calibration assessment

v2-decisions.note: Decisions resolved by synthesis document (no user Q/A needed)
v2-decisions.decision: Ensemble strategies ARE factorial factors for biostatistics. Source: synthesis Part 1.2 LAYER C. NOT a question — resolved.
Per-volume data is the unit of analysis (N=23 per fold). Source: synthesis Part 3.2 + QA round 6. NOT fold aggregates.
K=1 debug uses per-volume replication. K=3 production uses fold as random effect. Source: synthesis Part 1.2 LAYER A + QA round 6.
Layer B post_training_method and recalibration are ANOVA factors. Source: synthesis Part 1.2 LAYER B. New MLflow runs inherit upstream tags.
Zero-shot baselines excluded from factorial ANOVA but included in pairwise comparisons. Source: synthesis Part 1.2 zero-shot baselines section.
ANOVA is LAYERED (not flat N-way): core 3-way -> extended 5-way -> ensemble separately. Source: synthesis Part 1.4 + Part 7.1. Ensemble analyzed separately due to non-independence.
Full factorial is 720 conditions (production), 384 conditions (debug). NOT 24. Source: pre-gcp-master-plan.xml line 16 + synthesis Part 1.3 + Part 7.1.
Factorial YAML is COMPOSABLE via Hydra config groups. paper_full.yaml and debug.yaml are example named configs. Factor names auto-derived from YAML. Source: synthesis Part 1.3.
Only Layer A costs GPU money (~$65). Layers B+C run on local CPU (free). Source: synthesis Part 1.3 + Part 7.1.
